name: Millennial Olive Trees Trail
name_original: Los Olivos Milenarios — Circular desde Canet lo Roig
description: Hike the challenging 11.0 miles (17.8 km) Millennial Olive Trees circular route from Canet lo Roig, exploring ancient olive groves.
region_intro: Hiking around Canet Lo Roig offers diverse landscapes characterized by ancient olive groves and distinct rock formations. The region features a network of trails that traverse agricultural areas and natural terrain. Elevation changes vary, with some routes including moderate climbs and others remaining relatively flat. The area provides a tranquil environment for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails in Canet Lo Roig The most popular hiking route is Mola de Xert Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores the striking rock formations of La Mola de Xert. Another top favourite among local hikers is Millennial Olive Trees Trail, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.8 km) path. This trail leads through extensive groves of ancient olive trees, some dating back centuries. Local hikers also love the La Mola de Xert – Font de l'Aubelló loop from Xert, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail leading through the Mola de Xert area, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking around Canet Lo Roig is defined by ancient olive groves, unique rock formations like La Mola de Xert, and tranquil rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Canet Lo Roig are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Canet Lo Roig's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Canet Lo Roig?
A: Canet Lo Roig offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Canet Lo Roig?
A: The terrain around Canet Lo Roig is diverse, characterized by extensive ancient olive groves and striking rock formations, particularly around La Mola de Xert. You'll find paths traversing agricultural areas, tranquil rural tracks, and some routes with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Canet Lo Roig has over 30 easy hiking trails. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert – Font de l'Aubelló loop from Xert&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.6 km long and typically takes under two hours to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Canet Lo Roig?
A: Many of the trails in Canet Lo Roig are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880363328/mola-de-xert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Xert Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 12.2 km circular route exploring the unique rock formations.

Q: What are the most unique natural features to see while hiking here?
A: Canet Lo Roig is renowned for its extensive groves of &lt;strong&gt;Millennial Olive Trees&lt;/strong&gt;, some dating back to 569 AD. The region boasts the largest number of these ancient trees in the Valencian Community. Another striking feature is &lt;strong&gt;La Mola de Xert&lt;/strong&gt;, an area with dramatic, abruptly rising rock formations and vertical walls.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to visit on a hike?
A: Yes, you can incorporate historical sites into your hikes. The largely ruined &lt;strong&gt;Molino de Guiot Ruins&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Molino de la Abella&lt;/strong&gt; are interesting places near La Sénia. For a longer loop that includes these, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Guiot Ruins – Molino de la Abella loop from la Sénia&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/662867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Maestre Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Canet Lo Roig?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique tranquility and silence of the natural surroundings, the diverse landscapes featuring ancient olive groves, and the well-maintained network of trails suitable for various abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880318898/millennial-olive-trees-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Trees Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17.8 km path that takes approximately five hours to complete, leading through extensive groves of these ancient trees.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: While specific wildlife sightings can vary, the tranquil rural paths and olive groves provide habitats for various bird species and small mammals typical of Mediterranean agricultural and natural landscapes. The quiet environment makes it an ideal place for observing local fauna.

Q: Are there any places to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or explore religious sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel and Fountain of Sant Vicent&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Fountain of Health of Traiguera&lt;/a&gt;, all within the broader region.

Q: Is Canet Lo Roig a good destination for a peaceful hiking experience?
A: Absolutely. Visitors frequently highlight the unique tranquility and silence that pervades the natural surroundings of Canet Lo Roig. It's an ideal escape for those seeking peace and quiet during their outdoor excursions, away from bustling crowds.
tips: A unique place for its tranquility and silence that reigns all the time. Although it is neglected and mostly in ruins, it is an interesting place to visit just a few km from the town of Canet lo Roig.

It is a thousand-year-old specimen within the Canet lo Roig estate that has the largest number of the entire Valencian Community (1131 specimens).
Some of the olive trees were studied by the Polytechnic University of Madrid, which determined their date of planting in the year 569 AD.
Among so many ancient olive trees, there is one that stands out and it is the one nicknamed "4 legs" that has even won an award for its shape and beauty and that is also related to war stories serving as a refuge.
Definitely a place to visit with the whole family!

faqs.question: How challenging is this hike?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult hike, covering 17.8 km with an elevation gain of 370 meters. It typically takes around 5 hours to complete. While some sections might be easier, the full circular route requires good to very good fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including approximately 9.75 km of unpaved paths, 2.93 km of paved sections, and some portions categorized as 'road' (3.00 km) and 'street' (920 m). Expect some rocky trails, especially after initial asphalt sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the extraordinary concentration of millennial olive trees, some dating back to 314 AD. Look out for specific monumental trees like the famous Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig). You'll also pass the Hermitage of Santa Isabel, which, though mostly in ruins, offers a tranquil stop.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are typically spring or autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, avoiding the hot and dry summer months when shade is limited. Autumn is particularly special as you might witness the olive harvesting.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the unique landscape with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and agricultural areas you'll pass through.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The hike starts circularly from Canet lo Roig. Parking is typically available within the town itself, allowing for easy access to the beginning of the route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found near the town center.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, 17.8 km distance, and 370m elevation gain, the full circular route is not recommended for beginners or young children. It requires a good level of fitness. Shorter, easier sections might exist, but the complete loop is challenging.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this challenging hike, pack plenty of water, especially since shade can be limited. Wear sturdy hiking boots suitable for varied and rocky terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is essential, and consider bringing snacks or a packed lunch for the 5-hour duration.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Canet lo Roig, you'll find local amenities, including cafes and restaurants, within the town. These are good options for refreshments before or after your hike, offering a taste of authentic Spanish lifestyle.

faqs.question: What makes the millennial olive trees so special?
faqs.answer: These trees are living monuments, with some specimens over a thousand years old, dating back to 314 AD. Canet lo Roig boasts the world's largest concentration of these ancient trees, primarily of the Farga variety. They are not only historically significant but still produce high-quality 'liquid gold' olive oil, offering a unique connection to history and nature.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of Carrer del Portalet, Carrer Monxetades, Carrer de l'Escola, and Plaça d'Espanya.

waypoints.name: Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)
